arts.ttl: Curtis Jones and Tino Livramento called into England squad
arts.sum: Interim boss Lee Carsley lost Kobbie Mainoo, Morgan Gibbs-White and Ezri Konsa from his initial selection.

arts.ttl: Liverpool, Newcastle stars added to England squad as Harry Kane injury update given
arts.sum: England announce that Curtis Jones and Tino Livramento have been added to the squad for October's Nations League fixtures, while also delivering an update on Harry Kane.

arts.ttl: Every England men’s player of the year as Palmer claims prize
arts.sum: Cole Palmer has been named England Men’s Player of the Year for 2024 after a breakthrough year for the Chelsea midfielder. Palmer made his England debut in November 2023 and has since won nine caps for the Three Lions, scoring two goals. arts.ttl: Man United injury news: How long will Harry Maguire be out?
arts.sum: Manchester United centre-back Harry Maguire will be missing for "a few weeks" with the injury that he suffered during Sunday's Premier League clash against Aston Villa.

arts.sum: It's 16th December 2020. Arsenal have just drawn 1-1 at home to Southampton. They are six games without a league win, and the Gunners are 15th in the Premier League table. After the game,...

arts.ttl: Cole Palmer is named England's Player of the Year despite Chelsea superstar only starting two Three Lions games and playing 144 minutes at Euro 2024
arts.sum: Palmer has been in stunning form since making the move to Stamford Bridge in 2023, scoring 31 goals in 54 matches in all competitions.

arts.ttl: 'I play with Cole Palmer and he is very unique, phenomenal - the sky is the limit'
arts.sum: Palmer's Chelsea and England team-mate believes there is no-one in football quite like the attacking midfielder who was voted England Player of the Year by the fans

arts.ttl: John Barnes has advised Liverpool fans how to react if Van Dijk, Salah and Alexander-Arnold leave
arts.sum: Liverpool are under serious threat of losing some of their key players due to their contract situation. Virgil Van Dijk, Mohamed Salah and Trent Alexander-Arnold have all entered the final year of their contract at the club and their future remains uncertain at Anfield.
